threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep waiting for the bus on Kingsland Road like it’s going to arrive on time. Local folks know it’s a gamble at best.
Caught a late night 333 last week; a lad dropped his burger on the floor and insisted it was still fine to eat. The bus driver just shook his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the kebab shop owner and the café barista share a smoke break, laughing in the middle of a workday. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find snack stands selling currywurst and vegan döner side by side, catering to the fiercest food rivalries with zero shame.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays; they’ll just pull out a book instead of panicking like tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni held up by a dog on the tracks yesterday—classic SF. Everyone laughed, but you could see the frustration in some faces.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just the squares and bars; they miss the tiny art studios tucked into the side streets where locals create magic.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the 'tapa' ordering ritual—staring at the menu like it's a riddle instead of just diving in.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think London’s all about the tube. Try getting anywhere in Hackney after 11 PM without a bus or a taxi, mate.
Last Friday on the N35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old Michael Jackson tunes. It felt like a mini concert, the driver just smiled and let it roll.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor carrying an armful of vinyl records to a pop-up café, chatting about a hidden gem market around the corner.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves vegan doner with a side of homemade vegan tzatziki. It's a late-night lifesaver!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to pack snacks for BART delays—never trust the schedules, especially when there’s a loud bang at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched two tourists argue over a dog blocking the Muni tracks while a street musician played 'Don't Stop Believin'' on a ukulele. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but they miss the hidden vinyl shops and the locals-only bars down hidden alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they stand in the middle of the sidewalk, oblivious to the flow of locals weaving around them.
